findings: Jamba is a hybrid Transformer-Mamba-MoE architecture: interleaves Transformer and Mamba blocks at a tunable ratio (released config: 1:7 attention:mamba)
Combines benefits: Transformer in-context retrieval + Mamba's linear-in-length throughput and small memory footprint
Fits a 12B-active / 52B-total MoE model in a single 80GB GPU; supports 256K-token context with strong long-context evaluation results
Outperforms vanilla Transformer on standard LM benchmarks AND long-context evaluations at matched compute
Confirms a key tradeoff: pure Mamba still lags pure attention on certain retrieval-heavy tasks at matched scale; the hybrid recovers retrieval quality while keeping throughput
Cites first production-grade Attention-SSM hybrid; releases checkpoints + ablations
limitations: Ablations show hybrid ratio matters — too few attention layers hurts retrieval, too many erodes throughput benefits. Single-vendor release (AI21); independent reproductions still maturing.
relevance: Direct comparative evidence: pure-Mamba vs pure-Transformer vs hybrid on identical benchmarks. Strong support for the hybrid synthesis position.
